page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. This configuration includes 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, 128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core, and a 262 GB local storage drive. The CPU cache architecture consists of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instructions.

Benchmark evaluations indicate balanced performance across most workloads. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core CPU performance falls in the average tier. Memory performance is highlighted by a strong PassMark memory score, though raw memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average category. In application-specific tests, the server demonstrates strong image processing capabilities and top-tier text generation speeds for small language models with 135M parameters. However, prompt processing for larger models, such as the 70B parameter model, is weak, and no data is available for large-model text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32as_v4 offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a viable option for general-purpose workloads that do not require specialized GPU acceleration. The inclusion of 262 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability supports disk-bound operations. This server is suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale text processing or small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 32 vCPUs running at 3.525 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. It excels in image processing and text generation for small language models, though performance drops significantly for larger models. Lacking GPU acceleration, the D32as_v4 is a cost-efficient, balanced option for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 128 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage, delivering balanced performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, offering balanced compute and memory performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, solid memory performance, and local storage for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM
Features 262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language models
Best suited for general-purpose applications, databases, and web serving
